data.name: Django
data.summary: A comfortable, no-attitude French brasserie that was a longtime staple of the downtown Des Moines dining scene for nearly two decades.

data.name: Panka Peruvian
data.summary: Authentic and elevated Peruvian cuisine that provided a warm, welcoming, and highly-rated space on Ingersoll Avenue.

data.name: Americana
data.summary: Iconic downtown spot known for its sprawling weekend bombshell brunch and expansive views of the Pappajohn Sculpture Park.

data.name: Cactus Bob's BBQ Corral
data.summary: Long-running neighborhood joint known for smoked meats, baked beans, and a laid-back, western-themed dining room.

data.name: Palms DSM
data.summary: Upscale African-Caribbean style restaurant taking the metro by storm with dishes like braised oxtail and honey suya wings.

data.name: Scoops by Beth
data.summary: Modern ice cream parlor in Highland Park offering inventive, small-batch flavors in a beautifully lit brick-and-mortar space.

data.name: Bar Martinez
data.summary: Sophisticated lounge serving modern twists on classic cocktails in the historic Highland Park neighborhood.

data.name: Masao
data.summary: Unique French-Japanese fusion spot in the East Village, offering a refined blend of sushi, plates, and pastries.

data.name: Lachele's Fine Foods and the Fitz
data.summary: Retro-inspired eatery delivering highly updated, locally-sourced, and elevated takes on classic American diner fare.

data.name: Highland Underground
data.summary: Groovy '70s-style lounge offering fondue and craft cocktails in a nostalgic, cozy basement setting.

data.name: Toyo Ramen & Japanese Street Foods
data.summary: Fast-casual authentic Japanese ramen, gyoza, and donburi bowls in the District at Prairie Trail.

data.name: Black Cat Ice Cream (Downtown)
data.summary: Second location of the beloved handcrafted, small-batch artisanal ice cream shop utilizing grass-fed dairy and local ingredients.

data.name: Kura Sushi
data.summary: Interactive conveyor-belt sushi bar experience arriving at Jordan Creek Town Center.

data.name: The Breakfast Club (Waukee)
data.summary: Sweet and savory breakfast and brunch spot with a signature vintage comic book-themed décor expanding to Kettlestone Central.

data.name: Bubbies BBQ
data.summary: Slow-smoked meats and all the fixings expanding the culinary scene in Pleasant Hill.

data.name: Table 128
data.summary: Beloved elevated American dining that successfully reopened in a fresh downtown location after previously closing its West Des Moines spot.

data.name: Prime & Providence
data.summary: Upscale dining experience focused on prime steaks, wood-fired dishes, and exceptional service in West Des Moines.
